preamble: The shift from the mid-back rounded vowel [o] to the high-front rounded vowel [y] is part of a systemic chain shift involving both raising and fronting. It is most famously seen in the 'Great Fronting' of back vowels in French and Dutch, where the series [o] > [u] > [y] pushed or pulled the inventory forward.

examples.note: Latin long vowels shifted forward; the /o/ series raised to /u/ before some varieties further fronted to /y/ or /ø/.
Spontaneous fronting of the back series, causing mid vowels to raise and high vowels to front.
In Northern English and Scots, long /o/ fronted to /ø/ or /y/ rather than raising to /u/ as in the South.
